description: Head-receipt verification MUST fail when as_of is in the future relative to the consumer's clock beyond a small skew allowance (RFC-ACDP-0011 §7 step 6; RECOMMENDED allowance 120 seconds). A future as_of is a forged freshness claim: it would let a registry (or a replaying attacker) pre-mint receipts that still look fresh long after the head has moved. This fixture's receipt is VALIDLY SIGNED by the registry test key — the failure is the as_of check, not the cryptography. Signature values generated by temp/gen-0.3.0-vectors.py.

input.scenario: Consumer (clock: 2026-07-04T09:00:00Z) verifies a head receipt whose as_of reads 2036-01-01T00:00:00.000Z — nearly a decade in the future, far beyond any skew allowance.
input.receipt_hash_note: The preimage hash of this receipt is sha256:a7a45f2d2be5a22991cea858a17301e7632532c420f736796e8d468b70425106 and the signature verifies under the registry test key — implementations MUST still reject on step 6.

expected.behavior: Steps 1–5 of RFC-ACDP-0011 §7 pass (closed parse, signature verifies, bindings hold). Step 6 fails: as_of (2036-01-01T00:00:00.000Z) is later than consumer_clock + skew_allowance (2026-07-04T09:02:00.000Z). The head receipt MUST be reported as failed (invalid_receipt category). Contrast the non-future STALE case: an as_of in the past is well-formed and verifiable — its acceptance is the consumer's max-age freshness policy (RFC-ACDP-0011 §6, RECOMMENDED default 300 seconds), reported as a freshness verdict distinct from verification failure.
expected.boundary: An as_of within the skew allowance (e.g. consumer_clock + 60s) MUST NOT fail step 6 — honest clock skew between registry and consumer is expected.
expected.verdict_independence: Body and RFC-ACDP-0010 receipt verdicts independent, as always (RFC-ACDP-0011 §7).
